title: sedum hirsutum subsp. hirsutum
intro: Sedum hirsutum subsp. hirsutum, also known as Rough-leaved Stonecrop, is a succulent plant species that belongs to the Crassulaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood sugar levels, lowering cholesterol, and exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Sedum hirsutum subsp. hirsutum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
